articles.title: Takarazuka Revue Announces "Boys Over Flowers II" and "RRR" Sequel
articles.description: Everyone's favorite all-female Japanese musical theatre troupe has announced the Star Troupe's next two big musicals - "Boys Over Flowers II - Shooting STAR" (a new adaptation of the manga following their 2019 production from Flower Troupe starring Yuzuka Rei) and "RRR x TAKA"R"AZUKA -√Rama-", a two-act sequel to Star Troupe's 2024 "RRR × TAKA"R"AZUKA~√Bheem~" (which condensed the entire movie into one act, but S.S. Rajamouli attended and gave his approval). "RRR x TAKA"R"AZUKA -√Rama-" will be a two-act adaptation of "RRR", except with Rama as the main character instead of Bheem. The stars of 2024's "RRR", Rei Makoto and Maisora Hitomi, retired from the troupe in 2025 and 2024 respectively, and new Hoshigumi Top Star Akatsuki Chisei (as of August 2025) originally starred as Bheem in the 2024 production. Based on the success of their original "RRR" adaptation, they have decided to bring it back and expand it, with the role of Bheem now played by nibante (second ranking otokoyaku - male role actress - in the troupe) Rukaze Hikaru and heroine Sita being played by Top Musumeyaku (female role actress, musume meaning daughter) Uta Chizuru. "Boys Over Flowers II - Shooting STAR" starring Akatsuki Chisei will be playing at the Misono-za Theater in Nagoya from June 9 to June 30, and also stars Uta Chizuru, Amato Kanon, Mitsuragi Kai, and Taiki Hayate (Rukaze Hikaru will instead be performing in her first dinner show during this period at the Daiichi Hotel in Tokyo and Takarazuka Hotel in Takarazuka). In Takarazuka, Hoshigumi will also be putting on "Two Pieces of Silver -The Plum-Branch Hair Pin" during this time, from June 12 to June 27, starring up-and-coming otokoyaku Kishou Kazuto. "RRR x TAKA"R"AZUKA -√Rama-" will run from August 29 to October 11 in Takarazuka, and October 31 to December 13 in Tokyo (followed by Flower Troupe's 11th adaptation since it's premiere in 1996 of one of the Takarazuka Revue's top musicals of all time, Elisabeth, starring Towaki Sea and Hoshizora Misaki). Also announced this week, Moon Troupe's Top Pair of Houzuki An and Amashi Juri will be retiring after "Artemis of the Heavens/Belle Epoque"; "Artemis of the Heavens" is a new musical celebrating the Apollo program in the 1970s, announced in conjunction with the Artemis II mission (side note - I really hope they invite Christina Koch for a show, that would be amazing). In case you are curious about the 2024 "RRR" adaptation, here is a promo video of it and the accompanying revue, VIOLETOPIA. SOURCE 2 3 4
articles.source: Livejournal: Discover Global Communities Of Blogge

articles.title: Vanessa Trump, 48, Reveals Health Battle
articles.description: Rich Storry/TGL / TGL Golf via Getty Images Vanessa Trump, President Donald Trump’s former daughter-in-law, has revealed she has been diagnosed with breast cancer. Vanessa, who was previously married to the president’s son, Donald Trump, Jr., shared in an Instagram post that she had a “personal health update.” “I’ve recently been diagnosed with breast cancer,” the 48-year-old wrote. “While this isn’t news anyone expects, I’m working closely with my medical team on a treatment plan.” Read more at The Daily Beast.

articles.title: ‘Messy, chaotic, funny’: inside the hilarious comedy about teen Muslim schoolgirls
articles.description: Proper Ladies is being compared to Derry Girls for its portrayal of a wild but relatable girl gang. Its creator opens up about trying to reshape the way Black and Muslim characters are used in TV It’s not every comedy that dares to feature a character trying to strangle herself with her own hijab. Yet the BBC’s Proper Ladies has caused a storm on social media thanks to its chaotic energy and sharply observed teenage dynamics, drawing comparisons to shows such as Derry Girls and Some Girls. “We saw our first fan edit and it had 100,000 likes,” says writer Sabrina Ali. “It feels like we made it.” Set in a faith school, Proper Ladies is a 10-minute short that follows four schoolgirls in detention, where friendships, rivalries and acts of rebellion unfold. Absurd, quick-witted and fast, it leans into the heightened logic of teenage life – where the smallest things escalate quickly and everything feels urgent. In one scene, a student delivers a dramatic monologue about setting off the fire alarm to conceal the fact that she used the staff toilets to defecate. Continue reading...

articles.title: Jeff Bezos Fawns Over ‘Melania’ With Deluded Take on Movie Flop
articles.description: Lionel Hahn/Getty Images Amazon founder Jeff Bezos offered desperate praise for his Melania movie and insisted the documentary about the first lady was a hit. In an interview on CNBC’s Squawk Box, the billionaire was pressed on whether his company greenlit the film, which was panned by critics and flopped at the box office , in order to curry favor with President Donald Trump. Bezos described the claims as a “falsehood that will not die” while touting the apparent success of Melania —a movie that cost a total of $75 million to produce and promote and made just over $16 million at the box office. Read more at The Daily Beast.
